parents.father_research_notes: Henry Terry, attorney of Enfield CT

person.prominent_name_reason: Brig. Gen. Henry Dwight Terry — Union Army, Civil War; organized 5th Michigan Infantry 1861; wounded at Williamsburg; Battle of Seven Pines; commanded Johnson's Island Prison Camp. 7th-generation descendant of Thomas Hooker through Abigail Hooker Hart → Sarah Hart Marsh → Abigail Marsh Ripley → Julia Ripley Terry.

notable.notable_blurb: Union brigadier general, Fifth Michigan Infantry

military_service.notes: Wounded at Battle of Williamsburg May 5, 1862. Heavy losses at Battle of Seven Pines (Fair Oaks). Promoted Brigadier General Jul 17, 1862. Commanded Corcoran's Division at Suffolk, Virginia. Commander of Johnson's Island Prison Camp, Jan 1864. Resigned Feb 7, 1865.

narrative_blocks.header: He took the banner at Fort Wayne
narrative_blocks.body: Handed his regiment’s colors at the farewell parade in September 1861, Colonel Terry answered: “God grant it may never trail in the dust.” He had built the “skeleton regiment” from nothing — 5:15 a.m. drills, gambling banned, weapons locked down.

narrative_blocks.header: Wounded at Williamsburg, a general by summer
narrative_blocks.body: The 5th Michigan absorbed heavy losses at Williamsburg — where Terry was wounded — and at Seven Pines; by mid-July 1862 he wore a brigadier’s star. The next spring Longstreet besieged his brigade at Suffolk.

narrative_blocks.header: He talked his own general into retreating
narrative_blocks.body: At Baltimore Cross Roads in July 1863, within striking distance of Richmond, Terry fed General Keyes’s fears of Confederates massing in their rear — helping persuade him to retreat. Dix and Keyes lost their commands; Terry ended the war garrisoning the Johnson’s Island prison camp, superseded and idle by May 1864.

Dix and Keyes lost their commands; Terry ended the war garrisoning the Johnson’s Island prison camp, superseded and idle by May 1864.

military_service.notes: Also Captain 9th Company, 8th Regiment Jul 1775. "A thorough gentleman, Christian and patriot."
